extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Both of these outputs are driven exclusively by the `elderly` input: rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets rec_drug_a := true when elderly, and rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true when elderly. No other rules touch rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b, and none set them false; the remaining three rules only affect rec_drug_c. Therefore any input with elderly=true fires both rules, making rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. With elderly=true and seizure_history=diabetes=peptic_ulcer=false: rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=true, rec_drug_c=false (no matching rule, default). The conjunction (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) holds, so the property is violated. The other three inputs are irrelevant to this conflict (set to false here).
